description: Cross-sector baseline compliance controls for South Africa v1. This pack is designed to be applied together with sector-specific overlays. Rules are evidence-driven and deterministic; absence of evidence does not by itself constitute legal non-compliance.

metadata.scope: South Africa-only baseline controls for document/document-set review.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official South African legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013 (POPIA)
primary_sources.issuer: Information Regulator / Republic of South Africa

primary_sources.name: Promotion of Access to Information Act, 2000 (PAIA)
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / Information Regulator

primary_sources.name: Consumer Protection Act, 2008
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / National Consumer Commission

primary_sources.name: Financial Intelligence Centre Act, 2001 and FIC compliance guidance
Companies Act, 2008
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / CIPC / dtic

primary_sources.name: Occupational Health and Safety Act, 1993
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / Department of Employment and Labour

rules.title: Privacy notice or POPIA processing disclosure evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks whether the document contains a privacy notice, POPIA notice, privacy policy, or equivalent personal-information processing disclosure.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Processing purpose, lawful basis, consent, or legitimate-interest evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks whether the document explains why personal information is processed and records a lawful processing basis, consent, contractual necessity, legal obligation, or legitimate interest.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: PAIA access-to-information or information-manual evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for PAIA manual, access request, information officer, or access-to-records evidence where applicable.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Cybersecurity incident, breach-response, or security-safeguards evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for information-security controls, breach response, cyber incident handling, security safeguards, or unauthorised access handling.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Consumer disclosure, complaint, or redress evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for consumer disclosure, complaint handling, refund, cancellation, fair-marketing, or redress language.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: FIC Act AML/CFT accountable-institution controls are present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for customer due diligence, RMCP, suspicious transaction reporting, cash-threshold reporting, sanctions screening, or FIC registration evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
